title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
content:
## Key Themes

- **Habit stacking**: Link new habits to existing routines
- **The 1% rule**: Small daily improvements compound into remarkable results
- **Identity-based habits**: Focus on who you want to become, not what you want to achieve
- **Environment design**: Make good habits obvious and bad habits invisible

## Favorite Quotes

> "You do not rise to the level of your goals. You fall to the level of your systems."

> "Every action you take is a vote for the type of person you wish to become."

> "The most effective way to change your habits is to focus not on what you want to achieve, but on who you wish to become."

## Chapter Notes

### Ch 1 — The Surprising Power of Atomic Habits
Habits are the compound interest of self-improvement. Getting 1% better every day = 37x improvement in a year. The key insight: focus on trajectory, not current results.

### Ch 2 — How Your Habits Shape Your Identity
Three layers of behavior change: outcomes, processes, identity. Most people focus on outcomes. The most effective approach starts with identity — decide who you want to be, then prove it with small wins.

### Ch 3 — How to Build Better Habits in 4 Simple Steps
The habit loop: cue, craving, response, reward. The Four Laws of Behavior Change map to each stage:
1. Make it obvious (cue)
2. Make it attractive (craving)
3. Make it easy (response)
4. Make it satisfying (reward)

## Personal Takeaways
Immediately applicable. Started habit stacking my reading habit onto my morning coffee routine. The identity framing is the most powerful idea — "I am someone who reads every day" vs "I want to read more books."
summary: Excellent practical guide to building good habits through small changes, identity shifts, and environment design. Highly recommended.
